direction: How does training data size affect cross-institutional generalization in CNN-based pneumonia detection from chest radiographs? Specifically, fine-tune ResNet-50 (ImageNet pretrained) on NIH ChestX-ray14 subsets of size N in {1k, 5k, 25k, 100k}. For each N, evaluate on the held-out NIH test split AND on the PadChest pneumonia-labeled subset. Report the generalization gap (in-domain AUROC minus external AUROC) versus N, run 3 seeds per N with mean +/- SD, and compare to a published reference (CheXNet-Rajpurkar2017 or comparable).

context.ceiling: Workshop track / short letter quality. Not Nature-tier. Per KICKOFF.md Section 1.
hard_rules: external validation on PadChest is required because the question claims generalization
3 seeds per N with reported variance
no hyperparameter tuning on the test split
baseline reproduction within tolerance is required before any 'novel' claim
weak-label provenance for NIH and PadChest must be acknowledged
